road_lines.notes: Yellow center lines are a strong indicator of Americas, Japan, South Korea, or Australia. White lines lean European or African.

bollards.notes: Bollard style is one of the most reliable country identifiers in GeoGuessr

gen1_blurry_low_res: older coverage — parts of Russia, Africa, Latin America
gen3_high_res_trekker: newer coverage, hiking trails, small roads
google_camera.notes: Camera generation and blur quality can narrow down era and region of coverage

blue_left_strip_EU: European Union countries — France, Spain, Germany, Italy, Poland etc.
license_plates.notes: Plate color is useful but often hard to see clearly in street view

country_specific_clues.Brazil: Often has PARE stop signs (not STOP). Lots of red laterite soil. Yellow and green colors common. Dense tropical vegetation on roadsides.
country_specific_clues.Japan: Unique red torii gates, vending machines on roadsides, very clean roads, yellow center lines, power lines running along roads.
country_specific_clues.South Korea: Blue-roofed traditional buildings, Hangul script everywhere, unique orange/yellow road delineators.
country_specific_clues.Australia: Yellow center lines, kangaroo warning signs, unique red-brown soil in interior, 'Give Way' signs not 'Yield'.
country_specific_clues.New Zealand: Green bollards, 'Give Way' signs, SH (State Highway) route markers, very green rolling hills, sheep.
country_specific_clues.South Africa: Unique road signs, multilingual signs (11 official languages), distinctive suburban neighborhoods, yellow lane lines.
country_specific_clues.Russia: Cyrillic everywhere, Soviet architecture, often very straight long roads through flat terrain or forest.
country_specific_clues.Mongolia: Incredibly flat treeless steppe, ger (yurt) structures visible, unpaved tracks, very sparse settlement.
country_specific_clues.India: Extremely colorful trucks, cows on roads, left-hand traffic, Devanagari script mixed with English.
country_specific_clues.Indonesia: Dense tropical vegetation, motorcycles everywhere, Bahasa Indonesia signs, close to equator terrain.
country_specific_clues.Nigeria: Right-hand traffic, English signs, tropical vegetation, distinctive bus stops, chaotic urban scenes.
country_specific_clues.Kenya: Left-hand traffic, Swahili and English signs, red soil, acacia trees, distinctive road signs.
country_specific_clues.Turkey: Mix of European and Asian architecture, unique road sign style, mountainous terrain, Turkish script.
country_specific_clues.Thailand: Thai script on every sign, Buddhist temples visible, tropical vegetation, left-hand traffic.
country_specific_clues.Colombia: Spanish signs, PARE stop signs, tropical and montane vegetation, colorful houses.
country_specific_clues.Peru: High altitude visible, Andean architecture, Spanish and Quechua signage, terraced hillsides.
country_specific_clues.Ecuador: Very green due to high rainfall, Andean scenery, Spanish signs, PARE stop signs.
country_specific_clues.Argentina: Spanish signs, pampas grassland, European-style architecture, distinctive yellow lane markings.
country_specific_clues.Chile: PARE stop signs, Atacama desert clues in north, Patagonia in south, Pacific coast terrain.
country_specific_clues.Mexico: Spanish signs, ALTO stop signs (not PARE), vibrant colors, desert in north, tropical south.
country_specific_clues.USA: Yellow center lines, green highway signs, distinctive red octagon STOP signs, mile markers.
country_specific_clues.Canada: Bilingual (English/French) signs in many areas, similar to USA but metric speed limits.
country_specific_clues.UK: Red phone boxes, left-hand traffic, distinctive white/yellow road markings, GIVE WAY signs.
country_specific_clues.France: Blue motorway signs, French language, distinctive red-roofed rural houses, plane trees lining roads.
country_specific_clues.Germany: Yellow-black bollards, Autobahn signs, German language, orderly infrastructure.
country_specific_clues.Netherlands: Very flat, cycling infrastructure everywhere, distinctive rectangular blue bollards, windmills.
country_specific_clues.Sweden: Swedish 'Väg' route markers, blue and white road signs, dense pine forests, very orderly.
country_specific_clues.Norway: Fjord scenery, Norwegian language, distinctive road signs with black on yellow warnings.
country_specific_clues.Finland: Similar to Sweden, Finnish language (very different from Swedish), lots of lakes and conifers.
country_specific_clues.Poland: Cyrillic-free but Eastern European architecture, Polish language, often Soviet-era apartments.
country_specific_clues.Romania: Mix of Latin-script language and Orthodox churches, often poor road quality, Eastern European feel.
country_specific_clues.Ukraine: Cyrillic script, very flat in east, sunflower fields, Soviet architecture.
country_specific_clues.Greece: Greek alphabet signs, Mediterranean vegetation, white-blue color scheme, Orthodox churches.
country_specific_clues.Israel: Hebrew and Arabic script, desert terrain in south, Mediterranean north, Star of David visible.
country_specific_clues.Egypt: Arabic script, desert everywhere, Nile delta green corridor, ancient monuments near roads.
country_specific_clues.Morocco: Arabic and French bilingual signs, medina walls, Atlas Mountains, red-ochre architecture.
country_specific_clues.Ghana: Right-hand traffic, English signs, West African tropical vegetation, distinctive informal markets.
country_specific_clues.Senegal: French language, West African style, distinctive pink/orange laterite soil.
country_specific_clues.Ethiopia: Amharic script (unique), very mountainous terrain, distinctive clothing styles visible.
country_specific_clues.Tanzania: Left-hand traffic, Swahili signs, East African savanna, near Kilimanjaro.
country_specific_clues.Uganda: Left-hand traffic, English and Luganda, lush tropical green, equatorial.
country_specific_clues.Singapore: Extremely clean, English-Chinese-Tamil-Malay multilingual signs, very urban.
country_specific_clues.Malaysia: Left-hand traffic, Bahasa Malaysia signs, dense tropical forest, mix of ethnicities.
country_specific_clues.Vietnam: Vietnamese script (diacritics), motorbikes everywhere, right-hand traffic, dense urban areas.
country_specific_clues.Philippines: English signs, left-hand traffic (Jeepneys), tropical islands, Spanish colonial heritage.
country_specific_clues.Cambodia: Khmer script, Buddhist temples, flat terrain near Mekong, French colonial remnants.
country_specific_clues.Sri Lanka: Left-hand traffic, Sinhala and Tamil script, tropical, near India but distinct.
country_specific_clues.Pakistan: Urdu/Arabic script, similar to India but Islamic architecture, drier terrain.
country_specific_clues.Bangladesh: Extremely flat, Bengali script, very densely populated, tropical.

meta_tips.first_look: Always identify the script/language first — it eliminates most of the world instantly.
meta_tips.driving_side: Look at which side of the road the car is on, or which side has oncoming traffic.
meta_tips.sun_position: Sun in north = Southern Hemisphere. Sun in south = Northern Hemisphere. Can help narrow hemisphere.
meta_tips.road_quality: Very poor roads lean toward developing world. Very smooth = Western Europe, Japan, Australia.
meta_tips.utility_poles: Wood poles = Americas, Japan, Korea. Concrete poles = Southeast Asia, parts of Africa. Underground = dense European cities.
meta_tips.google_car_blur: The amount of blur and image quality can indicate era of capture and therefore coverage generation.
meta_tips.shadows: Long shadows = winter or high latitude. Short/overhead = near equator or summer.
meta_tips.lane_markings: Solid white edge line, dashed center, and any yellow = very useful combo.
meta_tips.snap_meta: STOP vs PARE vs ALTO vs ARRET vs unique scripts on stop signs is immediately useful.
meta_tips.soil_color: Red laterite = tropical (Brazil, West Africa, Southeast Asia). White chalky = UK, France. Dark black = Ukraine/Russia chernozem. Sandy beige = desert regions.
